jokes.text: Actor shows up to motorcycle audition in Hell's Angels jacket only to find 30 actors in bicycle shorts
jokes.setup: Actor gets excited about 'biker' audition, puts on leather Hell's Angels jacket
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's for bicycle riding, not motorcycles - completely wrong costume
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with strong visual comedy. Well-structured setup/payoff with economical delivery.

jokes.text: Brad commenting on not hearing Larry come in because they were laughing so hard
jokes.setup: Larry walks in on his wife having a good time with another man
jokes.punchline: Brad's overly explaining why they didn't notice Larry, making it more awkward
jokes.explanation: Subtle cringe moment establishing Larry's jealousy and social awkwardness.

jokes.text: Larry declaring he'll get a 'heterosexual single woman' to play golf with
jokes.setup: Larry is jealous of Cheryl's male tennis partner
jokes.punchline: His solution is oddly specific and defensive - 'heterosexual single woman'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - overly specific and petty response to jealousy. The redundancy of 'heterosexual single' is brilliantly neurotic.

jokes.text: Larry insisting all men want to sleep with their female friends
jokes.setup: Cheryl defends her male tennis partner as just a friend
jokes.punchline: Larry's absolutist stance: 'You don't know anything about men'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry misogyny disguised as wisdom about male psychology. Character-driven but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Jeff spills coffee and someone immediately knows the 'club soda and salt' remedy
jokes.setup: Coffee gets spilled on someone's shirt
jokes.punchline: Restaurant worker has very specific, confident remedy with club soda and salt
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major running gag of the episode. Simple but effective setup for larger payoffs.

jokes.text: Randy quits as chef because 'it's frowned on when they poison someone'
jokes.setup: Randy is quitting the restaurant job due to pressure
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions that poisoning someone is 'frowned upon' for cooks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan understatement. 'Frowned upon' is hilariously mild language for poisoning customers.

jokes.text: Larry's dismissive 'He loves ketchup, so what? You can't go by him'
jokes.setup: Randy is upset that someone asked for ketchup on his food
jokes.punchline: Larry completely dismisses the chef's concerns about his cooking
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry insensitivity - completely missing why a chef would be insulted by ketchup requests.

jokes.text: 'A lot of cooks... it's frowned on when they poison someone'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Randy's cooking failures
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about poisoning people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan understatement - treating poisoning customers as a minor professional faux pas.

jokes.text: The club soda and salt actually worked perfectly
jokes.setup: Earlier we saw the club soda and salt remedy applied
jokes.punchline: Cut back to reveal it completely worked - stain is gone
jokes.explanation: Good callback that validates the remedy, setting up Larry's later confidence in using it.

jokes.text: Melanie and Ed acting like they don't know Larry and Cheryl
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl encounter friends at the tennis court
jokes.punchline: The friends act coldly distant, clearly upset about something
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the wedding gift storyline. The awkward politeness masks obvious hostility.

jokes.text: Larry and Cheryl realizing they never bought a wedding gift after over a year
jokes.setup: They're trying to figure out why their friends are acting weird
jokes.punchline: The dawning realization of their major social faux pas
jokes.explanation: Relatable social anxiety moment. The slow realization builds the cringe effectively.

jokes.text: 'Buy 'em a car'
jokes.setup: They need to get an expensive late wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely over-the-top suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and escalation. Two words completely capture Larry's tendency to overcompensate.

jokes.text: Larry complaining about Cheryl's grunting: 'It sounds like pigs fucking'
jokes.setup: Larry criticizes Cheryl's tennis grunting
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Shockingly crude but perfectly Larry. The specificity and vulgarity make it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Larry demonstrates the grunting: 'How do you like this? Would you like to listen to this, huh? Ungh!'
jokes.setup: Larry doesn't believe he grunts
jokes.punchline: He aggressively demonstrates the exact behavior he was denying
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry move - proving he doesn't do something by aggressively doing it.

jokes.text: Larry's passionate monologue about finding the perfect sock
jokes.setup: Larry is excited about something mundane
jokes.punchline: It's just socks, and he realizes mid-speech how boring it is
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsession with trivial details. The self-awareness ('Is this boring you?') adds another layer.

jokes.text: Cheryl's response about what she'd say if Larry found perfect socks
jokes.setup: Larry asks if his sock talk is boring
jokes.punchline: Cheryl gives the exact enthusiastic response he wanted, highlighting how he's not getting it
jokes.explanation: Smart comeback showing Larry what genuine interest would look like, emphasizing his self-absorption.

jokes.text: Cheryl mentioning Brad is in 'Tony and Tina's Wedding' she wants to see
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions wanting to see a show
jokes.punchline: Casually drops that Brad is in it, making her motives transparent
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Larry's jealousy. The casual mention makes her interest seem obvious and planned.

jokes.text: Larry's long pause and 'Oh' after learning Brad is in the show
jokes.setup: Cheryl reveals Brad is in the show she wants to see
jokes.punchline: Larry's delayed, loaded reaction showing he's processing the implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. The silence and simple 'Oh' conveys volumes about Larry's jealousy and suspicion.

jokes.text: Larry's argument about 'assumed we' vs saying 'I' when making plans
jokes.setup: Larry caught Cheryl making solo plans to see Brad
jokes.punchline: Semantic argument over pronouns to avoid admitting she wanted to go alone
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pedantry used to win an argument. The linguistic analysis of relationship communication is very Larry.

jokes.text: Larry's 'You are so busted' and negotiating sex for forgiveness
jokes.setup: Larry has caught Cheryl in what he sees as deception
jokes.punchline: Both openly acknowledge using sex as relationship currency
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest portrayal of relationship dynamics. The transactional nature is uncomfortable but realistic.

jokes.text: Cheryl spills cranberry juice just as Larry is about to get lucky
jokes.setup: Larry has successfully negotiated sex for forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by domestic crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing interrupting Larry's moment of triumph. Sets up the club soda callback.

jokes.text: Larry's manic excitement about using club soda and salt
jokes.setup: Cheryl spills cranberry juice on the rug
jokes.punchline: Larry abandons sex to excitedly try the stain removal trick
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with escalation. Larry's priorities completely shift from sex to stain removal.

jokes.text: Larry frantically searching for salt while time is 'of the essence'
jokes.setup: Larry needs to quickly apply the stain remedy
jokes.punchline: Treats it like a medical emergency with dramatic urgency
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag. Larry's manic energy and medical terminology ('time's of the essence') elevate the absurdity.

jokes.text: Cheryl's 'window of opportunity that has slammed closed'
jokes.setup: Larry is focused on stain removal instead of sex
jokes.punchline: Bluntly declares the romantic moment is over using business metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect line delivery with great metaphor. The business language ('window of opportunity') applied to intimacy is brilliantly cold and funny.

jokes.text: Larry telling Josh it's an audition when it was supposed to be just dinner
jokes.setup: They're at Josh's house to sample his cooking
jokes.punchline: Larry unnecessarily revealed it's a job audition, creating pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry move - creating unnecessary social pressure through his inability to keep things simple.

jokes.text: Larry asking for salt while at Josh's audition dinner
jokes.setup: They're eating Josh's food to evaluate him as a chef
jokes.punchline: Larry asks for salt, potentially insulting the chef during his audition
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback to salt theme while creating cringe moment - asking for salt insults the chef during his audition.

jokes.text: Larry's dismissive 'Eh' when asked about the food
jokes.setup: Jeff loves Josh's cooking and asks Larry's opinion
jokes.punchline: Larry gives the most dismissive possible response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one sound conveys complete dismissal. Pure Larry contrarianism in its most concentrated form.

jokes.text: 'You weigh eight pounds. What do you know about food?'
jokes.setup: Larry criticizes Jeff's food judgment
jokes.punchline: Jeff attacks Larry's credibility based on his weight
jokes.explanation: Great insult with exaggerated number. The logic (thin people don't know food) is absurd but feels emotionally true.

jokes.text: 'You weigh eight pounds. What do you know about food?'
jokes.setup: Jeff questions Larry's food judgment
jokes.punchline: Larry's logic about thin people and food knowledge
jokes.explanation: Absurd exaggeration that attacks Jeff's credibility through his size.

jokes.text: Larry's bizarre sex analogy about food appreciation
jokes.setup: Larry defends thin people knowing about food
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate comparison to sexual experience and appreciation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - taking a food argument into completely inappropriate sexual territory with total confidence.

jokes.text: Larry calling Josh's food 'saucy' as criticism
jokes.setup: Larry has to explain what was wrong with Josh's cooking
jokes.punchline: Uses 'saucy' as if it's obviously a negative trait
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry critique - vague, unhelpful, and treating a neutral descriptor as obviously negative.

jokes.text: Larry still ordering dessert after rejecting Josh as chef
jokes.setup: Josh asks if dessert is 'out of the question' after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Larry still wants dessert despite crushing Josh's dreams
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry obliviousness - completely missing the social inappropriateness while focusing on his wants.

jokes.text: Larry complaining about not having a personality in the passenger seat
jokes.setup: Larry doesn't want Cheryl to drive
jokes.punchline: Claims the passenger seat makes him feel 'dull' and personality-less
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry neurosis - linking seating position to personality and identity. Pure psychological absurdity.

jokes.text: Brad giving Cheryl an Al Green tape with 'You oughta be with me'
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Brad gave Cheryl a music tape
jokes.punchline: The song lyrics perfectly match Larry's suspicions about Brad's intentions
jokes.explanation: Perfect confirmation of Larry's paranoia. The song lyrics are too on-the-nose to be coincidental.

jokes.text: 'Every day, this story is getting worse and worse and worse.'
jokes.setup: Each new detail about Brad confirms Larry's suspicions
jokes.punchline: Larry's mounting paranoia and frustration
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's spiral of paranoid thinking with each new 'evidence.'

jokes.text: Store clerk aggressively pushing napkin rings at 10% off
jokes.setup: Larry is just browsing in a gift store
jokes.punchline: Clerk becomes increasingly pushy with irrelevant sales pitches
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's explosion. Captures annoying sales tactics that everyone recognizes.

jokes.text: Larry's explosion at the clerk: 'You've done a complete circle with me now in the store'
jokes.setup: Store clerk keeps following Larry around
jokes.punchline: Larry precisely describes the stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Great Larry rant with specific observational details. The geometric description ('complete circle') is perfectly pedantic.

jokes.text: 'You've never heard of browsers? Is that word unfamiliar to you, a browser?'
jokes.setup: Clerk questions why Larry is in the store if not buying
jokes.punchline: Larry's condescending explanation of basic retail concepts
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry condescension - treating basic concepts as foreign while being maximally insulting.

jokes.text: 'Larry's in the car' - Cheryl announcing Larry's presence on speakerphone
jokes.setup: Brad calls Cheryl while she's with Larry
jokes.punchline: Her announcement sounds like a warning about potentially inappropriate conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect line that can be interpreted innocently or suspiciously. Larry's paranoia makes it seem like a warning.

jokes.text: Store clerk calling police claiming Larry is following her
jokes.setup: Larry had an argument with pushy store clerk
jokes.punchline: She flips the script and claims HE was stalking HER
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and reversal. The victim becomes the accused in perfect Curb fashion.

jokes.text: 'You can't give a gift after a year? What happens after a year?'
jokes.setup: The Loebs refuse the late wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Larry questions the arbitrary social rule with genuine confusion
jokes.explanation: Great Larry moment questioning arbitrary social conventions. His genuine confusion makes the rule seem absurd.

jokes.text: Larry's itemized accounting: 'You're into us now for about $5,500, as I see it'
jokes.setup: The Loebs are refusing the wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Larry calculates total wedding-related expenses like a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry - turning friendship into accounting. The precision of '$5,500' and 'as I see it' makes it brilliantly transactional.

jokes.text: 'Do you have anybody in there to clean up the mess I'm about to make?'
jokes.setup: Larry is furious about the gift rejection
jokes.punchline: Threatens violence while politely asking about cleanup crew
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line combining threat with courtesy. The polite phrasing of violent intent is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: Larry trying to break character by calling Brad by name during the interactive theater
jokes.setup: Brad is in character as Angelo during the interactive show
jokes.punchline: Larry tries to cut through the performance by using his real name
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about interactive theater. Larry's discomfort with performance leads to boundary-breaking.

jokes.text: Brad/Angelo's crude talk about Tina and 'slipping her the sausage'
jokes.setup: Brad is playing a character who's in love with the bride
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude sexual reference in context of interactive wedding show
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that works in context of sleazy character but isn't particularly clever. Serves the plot more than being funny.

jokes.text: Larry frantically calling for club soda and salt when wine spills at restaurant
jokes.setup: Wine gets spilled at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately applies his newfound stain removal obsession in public
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback culmination. Larry's obsession with the remedy reaches its peak in a public emergency.

jokes.text: Store clerk's husband confronting Larry as the wine spills
jokes.setup: Multiple storylines converge - the store stalking incident and the wine spill
jokes.punchline: Larry faces consequences for earlier behavior while trying to help with stain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant convergence of storylines. Larry's good deed (helping with stain) gets interrupted by consequences from his bad behavior.
